utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi janet , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm good . uh , are you ready to get started ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , janet is a 33-year-old female , here for some , some joint paint . what , what joint's been bothering you , janet ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's been mostly , um , my knees , actually . i've been doing more stair type workouts , which i thought was , you know , building muscle , uh , on my knees and supposed to help .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but in turn it , it really has n't , so my knees have been really bothering me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and is it one knee versus the other ? or both equally ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's both equally .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and have you been having any other joint pain like , elbows or shoulder , or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , but i , i should mention that like , i had a rotator cuff repair about two years ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , but i have n't had any problems since that point .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and what shoulder was that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that was my right shoulder .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . and , um , any pins and needles in your feet at all ? any swelling in your legs ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , no , nothing like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , but it would be something though i look out for often , just because i have diabetes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and your blood sugars have been under control ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , they have been .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and have you had any fever or chills ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . any nausea or vomiting , chest pains , shortness of breath ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , but i do have high blood pressure , um , but i , i monitor that and i've been taking medication for that , and that has n't elevated , um , in any way since the joint pain .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . so , yeah , i see on here that you do take norvasc , five mg a day and you've been taking that every day-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , and , um , so let's go ahead and do a quick physical exam on you . so , hey dragon , show me the blood pressure . your blood pressure's actually pretty high today , so you might be a little nervous here , which is not uncommon .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , so , looking at you , your , your neck is nice and soft , there's no enlarged thyroid . your heart has a regular rate and rhythm . your lungs are clear . your abdomen is nice and soft , but looking at your knees , on your left knee you do have a slight erythema and edema . there is a small efusion present over your left knee . um , you have some decrease flexion and extension of your knee , but your strength in your lower extremities is good . uh , so let's go ahead and look at some of your results . i know that we did some x-rays when you came in .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the left knee x-ray . okay , so looking at this , this is an , this is a normal x-ray of your left knee , so you may just have a little bit of a strain going on in your , in your knees there .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , let's look at some of your labs , 'cause i know that you have the diabetes . hey dragon , show me the labs . so , here your white blood cell count is not elevated , so i'm not concerned about an infection or anything like that . um , hey dragon , show me the diabetes' labs . okay and , and your a1c is a little elevated , but it's not , it's not terrible , so i think for right now we'll just continue you on the current regimen . um , so my plan from you , for you in terms of the joint pain , um , i'd like to go ahead and , you know , we'll just send some autoimmune labs to work , work up your left knee pain and , uh , we'll work for you to do some physical therapy and i'd like to go ahead and give you anti-inflammatory medication to help with the pain . does that sound okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey dragon , order meloxicam 15 mg once a day . hey dragon , order a physical therapy referral . hey dragon , order an autoimmune profile . and then from a hypertension standpoint you , because your blood pressure is a little elevated today , i'd like you to continue to monitor it at home and , you know , send me a report in the next couple of weeks sh- , you know , giving me the , the blood pressure reportings . do you have a blood pressure cuff at home ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and then , um , we'll go ahead and order a lipid profile for you . hey dragon , order a lipid profile . and then for , from a diabetes standpoint let's go ahead and , uh , continue you on your metformin 500 mg twice a day . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you have any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . the nurse will be in soon . it was good to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nice seeing you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 33-year-old female presenting for joint pain.
Joint pain
The doctor asks the patient to identify which joints are bothering her.
The patient reports bilateral knee pain exacerbated by stair workouts.
The doctor asks if the pain is in one knee or both.
The patient reports the pain is in both knees equally.
The doctor asks if the patient has pain in other joints.
The patient reports a history of right rotator cuff repair two years ago.
The patient reports no ongoing problems with her shoulder.
The doctor asks about symptoms of neuropathy or leg swelling.
The patient denies pins and needles in her feet or leg swelling.
The patient notes she monitors for neuropathy symptoms due to her diabetes.
The doctor asks if the patient's blood sugars are controlled.
The patient reports her diabetes is well-controlled.
The doctor asks about fever or chills.
The patient denies fever or chills.
The doctor asks about nausea, vomiting, chest pain, and shortness of breath.
The patient denies nausea, vomiting, chest pain, and shortness of breath, and notes she has hypertension that is monitored.
The patient takes Norvasc 5 mg daily.
The patient's blood pressure is elevated in the office.
Physical exam findings: neck supple, heart regular, lungs clear, left knee shows erythema, edema, small effusion, and decreased range of motion with good strength.
Left knee x-ray is normal; diagnosis is knee strain.
Labs show no infection; A1C is slightly elevated but diabetes is stable.
Plan
The doctor asks if the proposed plan for knee pain is acceptable.
Order autoimmune panel, physical therapy, and meloxicam 15 mg daily.
Continue hypertension monitoring and send report in two weeks.
The doctor asks if the patient has a blood pressure cuff at home.
Order lipid profile.
Continue metformin 500 mg twice daily.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.

case_description: joint pain knees bilaterally; right rotator cuff repair;diabetes;hypertension
